title: Todo API
description: A small REST API for creating, listing, completing, and deleting todo items.
background: Teams need a minimal, dependency-light service to track todo items behind a stable HTTP contract.

goals.title: Offer full todo lifecycle
goals.description: Support creating, listing, completing, and deleting todo items over HTTP.
successCriteria: A todo can be created, completed, and deleted via the API
Listing returns todos in a stable order

goals.title: Persist todos durably
goals.description: Store todos in a relational database so they survive restarts.
successCriteria: No todo is lost across a service restart

goals.title: Maintain a versioned contract
goals.description: Expose a documented, backward-compatible REST contract.
successCriteria: Breaking changes are gated behind a new API version

requirements.title: Create a todo
requirements.description: Clients can create a todo with a title and optional notes.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the todo is persisted and returned with a generated id and createdAt

requirements.title: Complete a todo
requirements.description: Clients can mark an existing todo as completed.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the todo is marked completed and the updated record is returned

requirements.title: Validate input
requirements.description: Reject malformed payloads with a descriptive error.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a todo payload without a title
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request fails with a 400 and a field-level error

architecture: A stateless Node.js HTTP service exposing a REST API, backed by PostgreSQL for persistence. Requests are validated at the edge before reaching the data layer.

inScope: Todo create, list, complete, and delete
assumptions: A single PostgreSQL instance is available

techStack.name: Node.js
techStack.rationale: Lightweight, ubiquitous runtime for small HTTP services.

techStack.name: PostgreSQL
techStack.rationale: Durable relational store with strong consistency guarantees.

folderStructures.description: Top-level layout of the HTTP service.

acceptanceCriteria.when: a client exercises the documented endpoints
acceptanceCriteria.then: each endpoint behaves per its requirement's acceptance criteria

nonFunctionalRequirements.description: Read endpoints respond quickly under nominal load.
nonFunctionalRequirements.measurementMethod: Load test with k6 against a staging instance

guardrails.description: Todo content must not be used to store secrets or personal data.
guardrails.rationale: The service has no encryption-at-rest or access controls.
guardrails.consequence: Storing sensitive data would create an uncontrolled data-exposure risk.

epics.title: Core todo endpoints
epics.description: Implement the create, list, complete, and delete endpoints with persistence.
epics.objective: Deliver a working todo CRUD API backed by PostgreSQL.

tickets.title: Implement POST /todos
tickets.description: Add the create-todo route with validation and persistence.

acceptanceCriteria.then: a 201 is returned with the persisted todo

implementationSteps.text: Insert the todo via the data layer

qualityGates: All new code paths covered by tests

tickets.title: Implement GET /todos
tickets.description: Add the list-todos route returning todos in creation order.

acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 is returned with todos in creation order

qualityGates: List ordering verified by a test

blueprints.title: Todo request flow
blueprints.description: Sequence of a create-todo request from route to database.
blueprints.content:
sequenceDiagram
  Client->>API: POST /todos
  API->>DB: insertTodo()
  DB-->>API: row
  API-->>Client: 201 Created
